data.body: PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility — overlay): PASS / not-applicable. The plan does not adopt any new external API/CLI/service/library, nor assert a capability it has not used before. Every external surface it relies on is already in production use in this codebase and verified present: the acli transport + Jira REST endpoints (e.g. `POST /rest/api/3/search/jql`, `get_comment_map`/`get_issuelinks_map`/`get_parent_map` in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py`, `get_issue_by_rest` in `acli.py`), the `links_applied`/`comments_applied` sub-op telemetry already emitted in `apply_outbound.py`/`apply_inbound.py`, and the existing live `jira-capability-probe.py`. Auth/HTTPS preconditions (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN) are already established by that probe and the live suite (`tests/external/test_link_sync_roundtrip_live.py`). The plan's strategy is deliberately hermetic (fixtures captured from real Jira, served through an in-process fake via the production fetch_snapshot path), with full live-sandbox e2e and cross-team Pact explicitly out of scope — so it adds no new live external dependency. Under T3's ANTI-FP clause (an internal, already-used integration is not-applicable), no findings.
